title: CLI settings integration, hook registration, and dispatch wiring
purpose: This brief wires the entire hook system end-to-end: load config from settings files, build ShellCommandHook instances, register them on HookRegistry, and wire all 6 new hook traits into their dispatch points in the agent loop. Loading without wiring is useless — both halves form one logical operation that makes hooks work at runtime.
task: Update norn-cli runtime builder to call load_hooks_from_settings during build_runtime, construct ShellCommandHook instances from the loaded HookEntry values, and register them on the HookRegistry. Then wire dispatch points for all 6 new hook traits: UserPromptHook at run_agent_step entry, StopHook at loop exit, SubagentHook in spawn.rs, CompactionHook in compaction.rs, PostToolFailureHook in tool_dispatch.rs, SessionLifecycleHook in norn-cli wiring. Handle the case where no settings files exist (no hooks, no error). Use NC-001's path resolution for locating ~/.norn/settings.json.

requirements.title: Load hook settings during build_runtime
requirements.spec: WHEN build_runtime executes, it SHALL call load_hooks_from_settings with the working directory to obtain the merged HookSettings. IF load_hooks_from_settings returns an error, the error SHALL be propagated. IF no settings files exist, an empty HookSettings SHALL be used (no hooks, no error).
acceptance: build_runtime or a new wiring function calls load_hooks_from_settings(cwd)
Uses norn::config::paths for ~/.norn/settings.json resolution
Error from load_hooks_from_settings propagates as BuildError
No settings files → empty HookSettings → no hooks registered → no error
Test: builder with no settings files produces a HookRegistry with 0 hooks

requirements.title: Build ShellCommandHook instances and register on HookRegistry
requirements.spec: For each HookEntry in the loaded HookSettings, the builder SHALL construct a ShellCommandHook with the entry's command, compiled matcher, validated timeout, and the appropriate HookEventType. Built instances SHALL be registered on the HookRegistry via Hook enum variants. Registration order SHALL reflect the 3-tier merge order.
acceptance: Each HookEntry produces one ShellCommandHook instance
HookEntry.matcher compiled via HookMatcher::new — invalid regex is a startup error
HookEntry.timeout converted to Duration
HookEventType determined by which field of HookSettings the entry came from
ShellCommandHook context populated with session ID, working directory, root agent ID, and profile name from the runtime bundle
Each ShellCommandHook boxed and wrapped in the appropriate Hook variant
All 13 event types mapped to their Hook variants
Registration order matches concatenation order from load_hooks_from_settings
HookRegistry installed on LoopContext.hooks (shell hooks append to any existing programmatic hooks, not replace)
Settings captured once at startup — no re-loading mechanism
Test: HookSettings with 2 pre_tool entries produces HookRegistry with pre_tool_len() == 2

requirements.title: UserPromptHook dispatch at loop entry
requirements.spec: WHEN run_agent_step begins, WHILE LoopContext.hooks is Some, the runner SHALL call run_user_prompt with the user prompt and session ID. IF the result is Block, the runner SHALL return NornError::HookBlocked with HookType::UserPrompt.
acceptance: run_user_prompt called in run_agent_step_inner before build_initial_messages
Block returns NornError::HookBlocked { hook_type: HookType::UserPrompt, reason }
Proceed continues normal execution
When hooks is None, dispatch is skipped
Test: register UserPromptHook that blocks, verify HookBlocked error returned

requirements.title: StopHook dispatch at loop exit
requirements.spec: WHEN the agent loop determines the model wants to stop (no tool calls, final text produced), WHILE LoopContext.hooks is Some, the runner SHALL call run_stop with the final text. IF the result is Block, the runner SHALL inject the block reason as a user message and continue the loop instead of returning.
acceptance: run_stop called after classifying the response as terminal (no tool calls)
Block causes the loop to inject the block reason as a follow-up user message
Block prevents AgentStepResult from being returned on this iteration
Proceed allows normal stop (AgentStepResult returned)
When hooks is None, dispatch is skipped
Test: register StopHook that blocks, verify the loop continues with injected reason

requirements.title: SubagentHook dispatch in spawn.rs
requirements.spec: WHEN a sub-agent is spawned via SpawnAgentTool, the runner SHALL call run_subagent_start with the child's agent ID and type. WHEN a sub-agent completes (in the finish function), the runner SHALL call run_subagent_stop. IF run_subagent_stop returns Block, the child SHALL NOT be marked as completed.
acceptance: run_subagent_start called in launch_child after the child agent is spawned
run_subagent_stop called in finish_child before marking terminal status
Block from run_subagent_stop prevents status transition to Completed/Failed
Agent ID and profile name (or agent type) passed to both hooks
Hooks retrieved from parent's LoopContext (if available) or from ToolContext extension
Test: register SubagentHook, verify start fires on spawn, stop fires on completion

requirements.title: CompactionHook dispatch in compaction.rs
requirements.spec: WHEN auto-compaction triggers, WHILE LoopContext.hooks is Some, the compaction logic SHALL call run_pre_compaction with the current event count. IF the result is Block, compaction SHALL be skipped for this trigger (not an error).
acceptance: run_pre_compaction called in maybe_auto_compact before the compaction event is created
Block causes maybe_auto_compact to return without compacting (Ok, no compaction)
Proceed allows compaction to proceed normally
Event count passed to the hook is the number of events currently in the store
When hooks is None, dispatch is skipped
Test: register CompactionHook that blocks, verify compaction is skipped

requirements.title: PostToolFailureHook dispatch in tool_dispatch.rs
requirements.spec: WHEN a tool execution produces an error output (output contains 'error' key or is_error is true), WHILE LoopContext.hooks is Some, tool_dispatch SHALL call run_post_tool_failure with the envelope and output. This is observational — no return value affects control flow.
acceptance: run_post_tool_failure called in execute_tool_call when the tool output indicates an error
Fires in addition to (not instead of) the existing PostToolHook
PostToolHook fires for all tool completions; PostToolFailureHook fires only for errors
Receives the same ToolEnvelope and ToolOutput as PostToolHook
When hooks is None, dispatch is skipped
Test: register PostToolFailureHook with counter, trigger tool error, verify counter incremented

requirements.title: SessionLifecycleHook dispatch in norn-cli runtime
requirements.spec: WHEN the norn-cli runtime creates a session, it SHALL call run_session_start with the session ID. WHEN the session ends (normal exit or error), it SHALL call run_session_end with the session ID. Both are observational.
acceptance: run_session_start called after HookRegistry is built and installed on LoopContext, before the first run_agent_step
run_session_end called during session teardown (in a drop guard or explicit cleanup path)
Session ID passed to both hooks
Both hooks fire even if no shell hooks are configured (trait hooks may be registered programmatically)
When hooks is None, dispatch is skipped
Test: register SessionLifecycleHook, run a session, verify start and end both fire

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify hook trait definitions — those are NH-001 and NH-002
SHALL NOT modify ShellCommandHook internals — that is NH-005
SHALL NOT implement settings file parsing or merge logic — that is NH-003
SHALL NOT change existing pre/post tool or pre/post LLM dispatch semantics
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: norn-cli builds and runs with no settings files (no error, no hooks)
norn-cli builds and runs with a valid settings.json containing hook entries
Invalid regex in settings.json produces a startup error
grep for 'run_user_prompt' in runner.rs returns matches
grep for 'run_stop' in runner.rs returns matches
grep for 'run_subagent_start\|run_subagent_stop' in spawn.rs returns matches
grep for 'run_pre_compaction' in compaction.rs returns matches
grep for 'run_post_tool_failure' in tool_dispatch.rs returns matches
grep for 'run_session_start\|run_session_end' in wiring.rs returns matches
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
